title: House Robber
description:
You are a professional robber planning to rob houses along a street. Each house has a certain amount of money stashed. The only constraint is that **adjacent houses have security systems connected**, so robbing two adjacent houses will trigger an alarm.

Given an integer array `nums` representing the amount of money in each house, return the **maximum amount of money** you can rob tonight **without alerting the police** (i.e., without robbing two adjacent houses).
solution:
# House Robber Problem
# Given an array of house values, find the max amount you can rob
# without robbing two adjacent houses.
#
# Approach: Dynamic Programming (optimized space)
# At each house, we decide: rob it (add to best from 2 houses back)
# or skip it (keep best from previous house).
# We only need the last two DP values, so O(1) space suffices.
#
# Time complexity: O(n) - single pass through the array
# Space complexity: O(1) - only two variables
#
# Key steps:
# 1. Track two variables: best sum excluding previous house, best sum including previous house
# 2. At each house, new best = max(skip this house, rob this house + best from 2 back)
# 3. Shift the two variables forward
# 4. Return the final best

def rob(nums: list[int]) -> int:
    prev2 = 0  # max money up to two houses back
    prev1 = 0  # max money up to the previous house

    for money in nums:
        current = max(prev1, prev2 + money)  # skip or rob this house
        prev2 = prev1
        prev1 = current

    return prev1
